mentions.note: Missed last quarter slightly and down 34% YTD; market doles out harsh punishment for quality companies not in AI/data-center; unsafe at any price level.
Gary caller: fiber optics plays all parabolic and extended; don't recommend buying these stocks up here; entire group needs pullback before next move.
Third-highest weighting in CIBR cybersecurity ETF (though primarily semiconductor/networking equipment); love it and owned by charitable trust for ages.
Yields ominous 6.66% indicating market deeply concerned about consumer durability; historically this yield level on retailer suggests real troubles brewing.
Pulverized after savage string of weak quarters; down 27% YTD despite being one-time excellent company; market despises anything not connected to tech and data centers.
Chris caller: quality company has fallen sharply but don't understand why; hold position, can't dump it on principle, can't justify buying more—uncomfortable hold.
Quality medical device company hit new lows; don't understand severity of fall but competitive pressures evident; unfair valuation punishment from AI mania rotation.
Michael caller: often-forgotten story; oil/gas recovery, infrastructure spending, plus engines powering data centers for hyperscaler gigawatt requirements; play on energy and AI; buy.
Mary caller: high-growth retailer but hurt by war narrative like all retail; can't tell you to buy until war ends despite reasonable fundamentals; geopolitical risk too overpowering.
One-time excellent company now worried about; yields 5%+ which on quality name signals market concerns about dividend sustainability despite historical strength.
Dividend yield of 7.56% indicates market fears dividend will be cut; these kinds of deals don't happen unless people worry about sustainability.
Double-bottom (W-pattern) chart is bullish reversal setup; explosive rally on strong volume and skyrocketing institutional money flow; could run to $600 by year-end, revisit $560s sooner.
Chugging like freight train with strong technicals and institutional buying; clearing $100 real soon on way to $110; made new ATH today so wait for earnings Wednesday pullback for better entry.
Worst performer in S&P today—illogical; ideal venue for less well-off when economy weakens; should be protected by defensive narrative not decimated by war fears.
Ideal shopping venue for price-conscious consumers given merchandise inflation; time-honored Wall Street proxy bought when economy deteriorates, not sold.
Missed quarter contributing to fast food sector weakness; entire fast casual class struggling in current war-environment consumer backdrop.
Phil caller: very hard to own with warranty problems and war impact; rates not going lower which was only thing Ford needed; pass on this.
Gets absolutely slaughtered despite selling 100% discretionary goods and being the strong performer of discount retail group; irrational selling on war fears.
One of most reliable stocks in market now hitting new lows with 7.2% yield; market hasn't caught into belief of dividend benefits despite high yield.

Unique satellite signals intelligence play with 74% revenue growth, explosive backlog, first-mover RF advantage over optical satellites; valuation 26x sales looks rich but justified by growth; can be bought at strong debut.
Market's go-to rate-cut play now eviscerated as rate-cut hopes fade due to war-driven oil and hot CPI expectations; needs housing turnover which isn't materializing.
Hit new lows with 5.8% dividend yield; elevated yield on quality food company signals market fears dividend sustainability; situation unfathomable for this caliber business.
Quality medical robotics leader hit new lows; victim of market's rotation away from traditional growth stocks toward AI/data-center plays.

Suffering from massive fire that wiped out Scott toilet paper production but about to merge with Capital View in fantastic deal expected to produce tremendous returns; merger benefits not yet reflected in stock price.
Susan caller: Cramer did extensive work on company; ready to roll; really really good stock; bull market for Eli Lilly—strong conviction buy.
Very tough currently, breaking down at 21x earnings with 2.7% yield; want to buy on yield basis if it reaches 3% yield for better risk/reward entry.
Medical device leader hit new lows; part of remarkable confluence of healthcare company punishments from market's AI rotation.
Hit new lows; market hasn't caught into benefits of McCormick's acquisition of Helmets business from Unilever; sentiment remains sour on entire food group.
Took 24% hit Friday on 20% workforce reduction and soft Q2 outlook but market overreacting; quarter wasn't that bad and layoffs done for efficiency not due to weakness; should buy here.

Cramer's favorite; owned for ages by charitable trust; strong uptrend with higher highs/lows from late Feb; bullish MACD crossover; extraordinary institutional buying; headed to $235 then possible $275-280.
Gave up post-earnings gains similar to 1999 pattern; momentum broken despite quality; facing same treatment as other mega-cap non-AI growth names.
No earnings momentum; buying only for 6.66% dividend yield which is too risky; if seeking yield, bonds are safer than dividend-dependent pharma.
Owned by charitable trust; tempted to buy more but no reason stock can rebound soon given current environment; rotation away from non-AI growth too powerful.
Bertha caller: data center/cloud company with great defense contract; multiple growth drivers; part of AI infrastructure layer cake—buy.
Burger King winning; better company than McDonald's; better positioned for current environment and consumer dynamics.
Respiratory care company hit new lows; suffering from broader healthcare sector decimation due to market's AI/tech fixation.
Had best numbers of entire discount retail group yet among worst S&P 500 performers today; head-scratcher; should be bought on weakness.
Medical device maker hit new lows; part of healthcare group slaughter as market rotates away from non-AI growth.
Had run hard pre-war, now experiencing huge reversal; profit-taking understandable but part of broader retail weakness from war impact on consumer.
Superb in weakening consumer environment; owned by Chapel Trust for years; can get merchandise from overburdened retailers at pennies on dollar; perfect inventory cleanout play.
Definitely a buy; before really good quarter at 25x earnings with great growth; great growth not just in data center but away from data center—exactly what market needs.

Excellent AI infrastructure power delivery story solving critical bottleneck; latest quarter strong, backlog excellent, margins improving; but already up 660% YTD and 60% this month—if own it take some off, if not wait for pullback.
Cut quarterly dividend from 25 cents to 14 cents (44% cut) and now yields 8%—unsustainably high level indicating market fears further dividend cuts ahead.
Had run hard pre-war, now experiencing huge reversal alongside Target; understandable consolidation but part of broader retail malaise.
Orthopedic implant leader hit new lows; suffering from market's rotation away from medical and diagnostic device companies.
Uber-consistent animal health company experiencing endless declines after good quarters; down 39% YTD, down 7.4% today; quality company but hammering unprecedented even relative to 2000 bear market.
